heading: stores
body:
```json
"stores" : [
  {
    "name" : "<string>",
    "provider" : "<string>",
    "allowInsecure": <boolean>,
    "urls" : ["<string>"],
    "defaultFormat" : "<string>"
  }
]

```

| Field                                              | Type    | Necessity | Description                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        |
| -------------------------------------------------- | ------- | --------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
| storesstores                                       | array   | required  | Array of objects where each object represents a data store to associate with the federated database instance. The federated database instance store captures files stored at publicly accessible URL (Uniform Resource Locator)s. Atlas Data Federation can only access data stores defined in the stores object.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  |
| stores.\[n].namestores.\[n].name                   | string  | required  | Name of the federated database instance store. The databases.\[n].collections.\[n].dataSources.\[n].storeName field references this value as part of mapping configuration.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        |
| stores.\[n].providerstores.\[n].provider           | string  | required  | s3 for an AWS (Amazon Web Services) S3 (Simple Storage Service) bucket.atlas for a collection in an Atlas cluster.http for data in files hosted at publicly accessible URL (Uniform Resource Locator)s.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            |
| stores.\[n].allowInsecurestores.\[n].allowInsecure | boolean | required  | Optional. Validates the scheme in the specified URL (Uniform Resource Locator)s. Value can be one of the following:true to allow insecure HTTP (HyperText Transport Protocol) schemefalse to only allow secure HTTPS (Secure HyperText Transport Protocol) scheme (default)If true, Atlas Data Federation:Does not verify the server's certificate chain and hostname.Accepts any certificate with any hostname presented by the server.WARNING: If you set this to true, your data might become vulnerable to a man-in-the-middle attack, which can compromise the confidentiality and integrity of your data. Set this to true only for testing and getting started with Atlas Data Federation.If omitted, defaults to false. If set to false, Data Federation returns an error similar to the following if a specified URL contains insecure HTTP (HyperText Transport Protocol) scheme:The insecure HTTP scheme is not supported by default - please add a "allowInsecure: true" flag to query from such URLs. |
| stores.\[n].urlsstores.\[n].urls                   | array   | optional  | Comma-separated list of publicly accessible HTTP (HyperText Transport Protocol) URL (Uniform Resource Locator)s where data is stored. You can't specify URL (Uniform Resource Locator)s that require authentication.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               |
| stores.\[n].defaultFormatstores.\[n].defaultFormat | string  | optional  | Default format that Data Federation assumes if it encounters a file without an extension while searching the databases.\[n].collections.\[n].dataSources.\[n].storeName.The following values are valid for the defaultFormat field:.json, .json.gz, .bson, .bson.gz, .avro, .avro.gz, .orc, .tsv, .tsv.gz, .csv, .csv.gz, .parquetIMPORTANT: If your file format is CSV or TSV, you must include a header row in your data. See CSV and TSV for more information.If omitted, Data Federation attempts to detect the file type by processing a few bytes of the file.The specified format only applies to the URL (Uniform Resource Locator)s specified in the stores object.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       |
